title: NNC Foundation Programs 2026: Scholarships, Community Grants, Literacy Support & Immigration Fee Assistance Opportunities in the United States
summary: The NNC Foundation (NamKil Noul Chung Foundation) is a registered 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization in the United States dedicated to transforming lives through education, literacy development, and targeted financial assistance programs. Through its structured initiatives, the foundation supports students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals facing immigration-related financial barriers. In 2026, the NNC Foundation continues to offer four active programs designed to promote educational equity, strengthen communities, and support vulnerable populations across the United States. These programs include scholarships for students, literacy and library development initiatives, nonprofit community partnership grants, and immigration filing fee support for employment-based applicants. Overview of NNC Foundation Mission and Impact The mission of the NNC Foundation is centered on empowering individuals and communities through: Access to education and academic opportunity Promotion of literacy and lifelong learning Financial support for underserved populations Strengthening nonprofit-community partnerships Reducing barriers in immigration processes for eligible applicants The foundation works with students, nonprofit organizations, and individuals in need, focusing on long-term social impact and sustainable development. Active Programs Offered by NNC Foundation (2026) The NNC Foundation currently runs four major programs , each targeting a specific area of social and educational development. 1. Global Domestic Scholarship Program The Global Domestic Scholarship Program is one of the foundation’s flagship initiatives designed to support academically strong students from financially challenged backgrounds. Program Objective This scholarship aims to reward students who demonstrate: Academic excellence Strong community engagement Leadership potential Financial need Scholarship Value High School Students: $2,000 award Undergraduate Graduate Students: $3,000 award Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet all of the following conditions: Must be a U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or have valid migratory status Must be currently enrolled in an accredited institution in the United States Must maintain a minimum GPA of 3.0 on a 4.0 scale Must demonstrate financial need and academic achievement Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Essay (PDF format) Most recent tax return (Form 1040) Academic transcript Recommendation letter Contact Help@nncfoundation.org 2. Literacy Library Programs The Literacy Library Programs are community-focused initiatives aimed at strengthening education through reading and knowledge development. Program Purpose This initiative focuses on: Enhancing language comprehension skills Promoting reading culture among children and youth Supporting local libraries and educational infrastructure Encouraging lifelong learning habits Key Impact Areas Local communities across the United States International educational outreach partnerships School-based and public library support systems Program Focus Literacy education development Library operations support Reading and comprehension programs Community learning enhancement Contact Help@nncfoundation.org 3. NNC Community Partnership Program The Community Partnership Program provides funding and collaboration opportunities for nonprofit organizations working at the grassroots level. Program Objective This grant program is designed to support organizations that address: Education inequality Literacy improvement Community health development Poverty reduction Social innovation initiatives Grant Focus Support for underserved and low-income populations Capacity building for nonprofit organizations Sustainable community development projects Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Be a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization Have a mission aligned with education, health, or community development Demonstrate at least 1–2 years of active community service experience Application Status Currently accepting applications Contact Help@nncfoundation.org 4. Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant The Immigration Filing Fee Support Grant provides critical financial assistance to individuals navigating employment-based immigration processes in the United States. Program Objective This grant is designed to reduce financial barriers by covering USCIS filing fees for eligible applicants. Key Support Features Direct payment of filing fees to USCIS on behalf of applicants Financial assistance for employment-based immigration petitions One-time award per beneficiary Eligibility Requirements Applicants must: Be a beneficiary of an employment-based immigration petition Be currently in the immigration petition process Demonstrate financial need Provide official documentation of the petition and USCIS fee instructions Important Notes Grant payment is issued directly to USCIS Only main applicants are eligible Each applicant may receive only one award Contact Help@nncfoundation.org About the NNC Foundation The NamKil Noul Chung Foundation is a U.S.-based nonprofit organization dedicated to advancing educational opportunity, community empowerment, and immigrant support services. Organization Details Status: 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Organization Address: PO Box #3301, Duluth, GA 30096, United States Phone: (470) 282-1643 Email: Help@nncfoundation.org Why These Programs Matter The NNC Foundation programs collectively aim to: Reduce educational inequality Support students from low-income families Strengthen literacy and learning infrastructure Empower nonprofit organizations Provide financial relief to immigration applicants These initiatives play a crucial role in building stronger communities and supporting long-term human development goals. Conclusion The NNC Foundation 2026 programs represent a strong commitment to education, community development, and immigrant support within the United States. Whether you are a student seeking financial aid, a nonprofit organization looking for funding, or an individual navigating immigration costs, these programs provide meaningful opportunities for assistance and growth. title: IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) Philippines Recruitment 2026: Latest Job Vacancies, Roles, Deadlines, and How to Apply
summary: The International Organization for Migration (IOM) continues to expand its global humanitarian and administrative operations through its Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC), also known as the Manila Administrative Centre (MAC). As part of its worldwide mission to support migration governance and humanitarian assistance, IOM is currently recruiting for multiple positions across administration, human resources, supply chain, healthcare, data analysis, payroll, and consultancy roles. All positions advertised under this recruitment cycle are based in the Philippines, specifically within the Manila Global Administrative Centre, a strategic hub supporting IOM operations worldwide. About the International Organization for Migration (IOM) The International Organization for Migration (IOM) is a leading intergovernmental organization working in the field of migration management. It operates globally to support migrants, governments, and communities through humanitarian response, development programs, and policy support. As part of the United Nations migration system, IOM emphasizes: Efficiency and high professional standards Integrity and accountability in humanitarian work Diversity and inclusion across its global workforce Equitable geographical representation in staffing Multicultural collaboration in all duty stations Working at IOM means contributing directly to life-saving operations, global migration governance, and humanitarian coordination across countries. Overview of IOM Global Shared Services Centre (GSSC) – Manila The Manila-based Administrative Centre serves as a global operational hub that supports: Human resources management Supply chain and logistics coordination Medical and health data services Payroll and financial administration IT, data analysis, and digital transformation Recruitment and talent acquisition This center plays a key role in ensuring that IOM field missions operate efficiently across continents. Latest Job Vacancies at IOM GSSC (2026) Below are the currently open positions, including deadlines and grades: 1. Asset and Inventory Assistant (G-4) Code: VN 047/2026 Deadline: 22 May 2026 Role: Manage inventory systems, asset tracking, and logistics documentation. 2. HR Associate (Ungraded) Code: CFA 005/2026 Deadline: 22 May 2026 Role: Support HR processes, recruitment administration, and employee records. 3. National Medical Officer (Ungraded) Code: CFA 006/2026 Deadline: 24 May 2026 Role: Provide medical expertise for staff health and humanitarian operations. 4. Human Resources Intern (Open to Fresh Graduates) Code: INT 007/2026 Deadline: 29 May 2026 Role: Entry-level HR support and administrative learning opportunity. 5. Supply Chain Associate (Master Data Management) (G-5) Code: VN 048/2026 Deadline: 29 May 2026 Role: Manage supply chain data systems and procurement support. 6. National Teleradiology Data Analysis Officer (NO-A) Code: VN 049/2026 Deadline: 29 May 2026 Role: Analyze medical imaging data and support remote diagnostics systems. 7. Senior Payroll Administrator (Ungraded) Code: CFA 007-008/2026 Deadline: 04 June 2026 Role: Oversee payroll systems, salary processing, and financial compliance. 8. National HR Officer (Talent Acquisition Regional Focal) (NO-B) Code: VN 050/2026 Deadline: 04 June 2026 Role: Lead recruitment strategies and regional talent acquisition processes. 9. SEO Specialist (G-6) Code: VN 031/2026 Deadline: 04 June 2026 Role: Optimize digital visibility, web content strategy, and search engine performance. 10. Radiologist (Teleradiology) Consultant Code: CON 011-018/2026 Deadline: 05 June 2026 Role: Provide expert radiology consultation through remote diagnostic systems. Key Features of Working with IOM GSSC Working within the Manila Administrative Centre offers: International exposure in a UN-related environment Multidisciplinary career development opportunities Competitive and structured global salary scales Experience in humanitarian and migration-focused operations Multicultural and inclusive workplace culture Opportunities for career progression within the UN system How to Apply Interested candidates are encouraged to apply directly through the official IOM careers platform by submitting: Updated CV/resume Cover letter tailored to the position Academic certificates and relevant professional documents Proof of experience (where required) Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Why This Opportunity Matters These vacancies represent a significant opportunity to: Join a UN-affiliated organization Contribute to global humanitarian and migration governance Work in a structured international administrative environment Build a career in HR, logistics, healthcare, data analysis, or digital systems Conclusion The IOM GSSC Manila recruitment drive for 2026 offers diverse career pathways for professionals and fresh graduates alike. With roles ranging from internships to senior consultancy positions, this is a key opportunity for individuals seeking international careers within the United Nations system. title: World Vision Brussels Careers 2026: Humanitarian Advocacy & Policy Specialist and Junior Communication & Policy Officer Opportunities in Belgium
summary: World Vision International has announced exciting career opportunities within its European Union Representation Office (EUREP) in Brussels, Belgium. These openings provide professionals and young graduates with the opportunity to contribute to global humanitarian advocacy, European Union policy engagement, child-focused development work, and international communications. The organization is currently recruiting for two important positions: Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist Junior Communication Policy Officer (Trainee) Both opportunities are based in Brussels and offer candidates the chance to work with one of the world’s largest humanitarian and development organizations while engaging directly with European Union institutions, advocacy networks, and global policy initiatives. These positions are ideal for individuals passionate about humanitarian affairs, development cooperation, communications, public policy, advocacy, children’s rights, and international relations. About World Vision World Vision International is a global Christian humanitarian, development, and advocacy organization dedicated to improving the lives of vulnerable children, families, and communities worldwide. Operating in nearly 100 countries with more than 31,000 staff members, World Vision works across areas such as: Child protection Emergency response Humanitarian assistance Poverty reduction Education Health and nutrition Advocacy and policy engagement Community resilience Development programming The organization collaborates with governments, institutions, NGOs, donors, and local communities to create sustainable and impactful solutions to global challenges affecting children and vulnerable populations. About the World Vision EU Representation Office (EUREP) The Brussels-based EU Representation Office supports World Vision’s engagement with European Union institutions and stakeholders. The office focuses on two major strategic priorities: 1. Influencing EU Policies and Decision-Making The office works to ensure that European Union policies, funding programs, and political decisions positively impact vulnerable children and communities around the world. This involves: Policy advocacy Humanitarian diplomacy Stakeholder engagement Campaign coordination Strategic communication Institutional partnerships 2. Facilitating Access to EU Funding Opportunities The office also supports World Vision teams globally in accessing EU development and humanitarian funding opportunities. As one of the largest international donors, the European Union plays a critical role in financing global humanitarian and development initiatives. World Vision’s Brussels office helps connect field offices and programs with these funding mechanisms. Position 1: Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist Job Location Brussels, Belgium Employment Type Permanent full-time contract Department Humanitarian Team Start Date As soon as possible (ASAP) Overview of the Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist Role The Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist will play a central role in supporting World Vision’s humanitarian engagement with European Union institutions. The selected candidate will contribute to: EU humanitarian advocacy strategies Policy analysis Institutional engagement Humanitarian diplomacy Funding environment monitoring Child-focused humanitarian action The role focuses heavily on humanitarian crises, resilience, fragility, and Nexus-related issues affecting vulnerable populations globally. The successful candidate will help strengthen World Vision’s visibility and credibility within European humanitarian policy spaces while supporting advocacy initiatives linked to children in crisis and fragile contexts. Key Responsibilities The Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist will undertake several strategic and operational responsibilities. Policy and Advocacy Strategy Responsibilities include: Supporting the development and implementation of EU humanitarian advocacy strategies Aligning advocacy efforts with World Vision priorities and ECHO priorities Coordinating humanitarian policy initiatives Humanitarian Advocacy Leadership The role involves leading advocacy actions concerning: Humanitarian crises Fragility Resilience Nexus-related issues Research and Policy Documentation The selected candidate will: Draft policy papers Prepare advocacy briefs Develop analyses and position papers Produce documents related to EU humanitarian affairs EU Policy Monitoring Responsibilities include monitoring: EU humanitarian policies Political trends Stakeholder developments Funding environments Stakeholder Engagement The specialist will strengthen relationships with: ECHO EU institutions NGOs Coalitions Humanitarian partners in Brussels Representation Duties The role also includes representing World Vision at: EU humanitarian events Advocacy forums Policy discussions Networks such as VOICE Coordination and Internal Advisory Support The candidate will: Coordinate advocacy visits and campaigns Support high-level engagements Advise internal teams on EU humanitarian advocacy priorities Facilitate alignment across global, regional, and field offices Qualifications and Skills Required Candidates applying for this role should possess strong academic, professional, and interpersonal skills. Educational Requirements Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in areas such as: International Relations Humanitarian Affairs Public Policy Development Studies Related disciplines Professional Experience Candidates should have: 2 to 4 years of experience in EU humanitarian policy and advocacy Technical Knowledge Applicants should demonstrate: Strong understanding of EU institutions Strategic thinking abilities Policy influence capabilities Advocacy coordination experience Additional Skills Required competencies include: Networking and coalition-building Project coordination Excellent organizational skills Ability to manage multiple priorities Strong communication abilities Language Requirements Excellent written and spoken English is mandatory French is considered an advantage Other Requirements Candidates should: Be willing to travel occasionally Support diversity, equity, and inclusion Commit to World Vision’s mission and values Already possess the legal right to work in Belgium Salary and Benefits World Vision offers an attractive compensation package for the Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist role. Salary Range €3,039 to €3,490 gross per month Paid over 13.92 months Additional Benefits Employees will benefit from: Permanent full-time contract Supportive work environment Inclusive and dynamic team culture Professional development opportunities Respectful workplace environment Competitive market-aligned compensation Application Deadline for Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist Interested candidates must submit their: CV Cover letter Applications should be sent to: eulo-info@wvi.org Subject line: “Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist” Deadline 31 May 2026 Position 2: Junior Communication Policy Officer (Trainee) Job Location Brussels, Belgium Employment Type Paid internship under Contrat d’Immersion Professionnelle (CIP) Department Advocacy Team Start Date As soon as possible (ASAP) Overview of the Junior Communication Policy Officer Role The Junior Communication Policy Officer position is designed for young professionals seeking experience in: Communication Advocacy Policy engagement Event coordination Digital communications EU institutional relations The trainee will contribute to communication activities and support advocacy and policy initiatives related to children’s rights and development priorities. This role offers increasing levels of responsibility and autonomy throughout the traineeship period. Key Responsibilities Communication Support The trainee will: Ensure communication outputs comply with branding and visibility requirements Support strategic communication initiatives Assist in campaign development Create communication materials Digital Communications Responsibilities include: Managing digital communication channels Updating websites Supporting social media activities Creating online content Monitoring and Analysis The candidate will: Monitor communication activities Analyze communication impact Support reporting and evaluation efforts Event Coordination The role also includes: Assisting with event planning Coordinating meetings Supporting advocacy activities Qualifications and Skills Required Educational Background Candidates should possess: A Master’s degree in Communication or related fields OR Relevant experience linked to World Vision’s mission Knowledge Requirements Applicants should understand: European Union institutions Development policies Child-focused advocacy Technical Skills Required competencies include: Digital communication skills Social media management Research and analytical skills Communication performance monitoring Policy analysis Language Requirements Excellent written and spoken English Personal Skills Applicants should demonstrate: Strong organizational skills Ability to meet deadlines Team spirit Capacity to work in multicultural environments Support for World Vision’s Christian identity and values Internship Benefits The Junior Communication Policy Officer internship includes several attractive benefits. Internship Duration 6 months Possibility of extension for another 6 months Additional Benefits Interns will receive: Competitive internship compensation Lunch vouchers 5 weeks of paid holiday Belgian public holidays Up to 20 days of remote work from abroad The organization also promotes: Personal development Team spirit Autonomy Inclusive workplace culture Application Deadline for Junior Communication Policy Officer Applicants must submit: CV Cover letter Email: eulo-info@wvi.org Subject line: “Junior Communication Policy Officer (Trainee)” Deadline 22 May 2026 Why These Opportunities Matter These positions provide exceptional opportunities for professionals and graduates interested in: European Union policy Humanitarian affairs International advocacy Child rights Development cooperation Global communications Working within World Vision’s EU Representation Office offers direct exposure to high-level policy discussions, international partnerships, and humanitarian advocacy processes that shape global development agendas. Candidates will gain valuable experience engaging with: EU institutions NGOs Donors Advocacy coalitions Humanitarian networks Final Thoughts The 2026 recruitment opportunities at World Vision International offer talented professionals and aspiring advocates the chance to build meaningful careers while contributing to global humanitarian and child-focused development efforts. Whether applying for the permanent Humanitarian Advocacy Policy Specialist role or the Junior Communication Policy Officer traineeship, candidates will join a globally respected organization dedicated to creating positive change for vulnerable communities worldwide. Professionals with strong backgrounds in advocacy, communication, humanitarian affairs, EU policy, and international development should strongly consider applying before the respective deadlines. title: Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature 2026–2027: A Global Opportunity Transforming Mental Health Innovation
summary: The global mental health crisis continues to affect millions of people across every continent, creating an urgent need for innovative, evidence-based solutions that can improve lives and strengthen healthcare systems. In response to this growing challenge, Wellcome, in partnership with Nature, has launched the prestigious Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature . Recognized as one of the world’s most significant mental health research prizes, this initiative aims to accelerate transformative interventions targeting anxiety, depression, and psychosis. The competition not only provides substantial financial support but also offers global recognition, policy visibility, networking opportunities, and practical assistance to help successful interventions move toward large-scale adoption. Applications are now open for the 2026–2027 edition, with one winning team set to receive an extraordinary US$1 million prize , while three finalists will each receive US$250,000 . About the Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature was created to recognize and support groundbreaking mental health interventions capable of producing measurable and lasting impacts on people living with mental health conditions. The prize specifically focuses on interventions addressing: Anxiety Depression Psychosis Mental health disorders remain among the leading causes of disability worldwide. Despite increasing awareness, access to effective treatments and scalable interventions remains limited in many regions. Through this initiative, Wellcome and Nature aim to bridge the gap between scientific discovery and real-world implementation. The prize celebrates innovation while also helping researchers and institutions overcome barriers related to visibility, funding, adoption, and policy integration. Main Goals of the Prize The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature was established with several ambitious objectives designed to reshape the future of global mental health research and implementation. 1. Accelerate Effective Mental Health Interventions The prize seeks to support interventions that demonstrate effectiveness in improving outcomes for individuals affected by anxiety, depression, or psychosis. This includes innovations related to: Clinical treatments Therapeutic models Community-based interventions Digital mental health tools Behavioral approaches Preventive strategies Public health solutions Technology-driven mental health systems The ultimate aim is to help these interventions move more rapidly toward widespread adoption. 2. Increase Policy Prioritization of Mental Health Another major objective is to encourage governments, institutions, and health systems to prioritize mental health within public policy and healthcare planning. Winning projects may influence: National mental health strategies Global health frameworks Healthcare investment decisions Research funding priorities Community mental health programs 3. Inspire Global Interest in Mental Health Science The prize also aims to increase public and institutional enthusiasm for mental health research by highlighting innovative scientific achievements capable of transforming lives. By elevating successful interventions, the initiative hopes to encourage: Greater public awareness Increased philanthropic investment Expanded scientific collaboration More funding for mental health research worldwide Prize Benefits and Financial Awards One of the most attractive aspects of this opportunity is its substantial financial support. Overall Winner The winning team will receive: US$1 million This funding is intended to help accelerate the implementation and expansion of the winning intervention. Finalists Three runner-up teams will each receive: US$250,000 These funds are designed to support further development, adoption, and policy integration of finalist interventions. Additional Benefits Beyond Funding The Wellcome Prize offers far more than financial rewards. Selected teams will also gain access to several strategic advantages that can significantly increase the impact of their work. Global Visibility Winning and finalist teams will receive international recognition through the extensive networks of Wellcome and Nature. Strategic Connections Participants may benefit from networking opportunities involving: Policymakers Researchers Healthcare leaders Investors Philanthropic organizations Global health institutions Practical Support The initiative also provides practical support aimed at helping interventions progress toward real-world adoption and integration into healthcare systems. Enhanced Credibility Being associated with internationally respected organizations like Wellcome and Nature can significantly strengthen the credibility and influence of participating teams and institutions. Who Can Apply? The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature is open to research teams developing innovative interventions related to anxiety, depression, or psychosis. Eligible teams may come from various sectors, including: Universities Research institutes Hospitals Government organizations Not-for-profit organizations Small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) Importantly, applications are welcomed from teams based anywhere in the world, making this a truly global opportunity. Eligible Research Areas Applicants may submit interventions related to a wide range of mental health innovation areas, provided they focus on anxiety, depression, or psychosis. Examples may include: Novel treatment methods Community mental health programs AI-driven mental health technologies Telehealth solutions Preventive mental health interventions Behavioral therapies Neurobiological research applications Digital mental health platforms Public health mental health strategies Early diagnosis systems Scalable healthcare delivery models Projects demonstrating strong scientific evidence and real-world impact potential are likely to stand out during the evaluation process. Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the following timeline for the 2026–2027 competition cycle. Event Date Applications Open 14 May 2026 Application Deadline 18 September 2026 Shortlist Announcement April 2027 Finalists Announcement May 2027 Winner Announcement June 2027 About the Organizers Wellcome Wellcome is a globally recognized charitable foundation supporting scientific research aimed at solving urgent health challenges worldwide. The organization focuses on: Mental health Infectious diseases Climate and health Life sciences Human wellbeing Wellcome invests heavily in discovery research and global health innovation initiatives. Nature Nature is one of the world’s leading scientific publishing organizations, known for advancing scientific discovery through high-quality research publications and authoritative journalism. Through Nature Awards, the organization supports outstanding researchers and promotes scientific innovation capable of generating real-world impact. Why This Prize Matters Globally Mental health conditions continue to rise worldwide, affecting individuals, families, healthcare systems, and economies. Despite increasing awareness, many innovative interventions struggle to obtain the funding, policy attention, and implementation support necessary to scale their impact. The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature addresses this gap directly by: Rewarding evidence-based innovation Encouraging policy prioritization Increasing global awareness Supporting scalable mental health solutions Accelerating real-world implementation For research teams working on transformative mental health interventions, this prize represents a rare opportunity to gain both substantial funding and international recognition. How to Apply Interested teams should prepare a strong application showcasing: The innovation behind their intervention Scientific evidence of effectiveness Potential for real-world impact Scalability and adoption potential Relevance to anxiety, depression, or psychosis Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications before the official deadline of 18 September 2026 . Official Website: Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature Final Thoughts The Wellcome Prize for Mental Health Science with Nature stands out as one of the most ambitious and impactful mental health science competitions in the world. By combining major financial support with global visibility and policy engagement opportunities, the prize aims to accelerate innovations capable of transforming mental healthcare worldwide. For researchers, institutions, hospitals, nonprofits, and SMEs developing effective interventions for anxiety, depression, or psychosis, this opportunity could become a powerful catalyst for scaling impact and influencing the future of global mental health.
